channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Eight students arrested in deadly Kenyan school dorm fire
item.description: Kenyan police have arrested eight students on suspicion of arson after a deadly fire ripped through a girls’ school.

item.title: EU to release billions in frozen funds for Hungary amid Magyar reforms
item.description: PM Peter Magyar called ​the agreement to unlock funds frozen under former leader Orban a 'historic' breakthrough.

item.title: Trump says ‘final determination’ to be made on possible Iran deal
item.description: Deep mistrust remains between Washington and Tehran as Iran's top negotiator urges action, not words.

item.title: Will the AI race fuel another boom or another bubble?
item.description: AI giants are racing towards public listings as valuations soar.

item.title: Vance says US, Iran have made “a lot of progress” towards deal
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance says Washington and Tehran have made “a lot of progress” towards ceasefire

item.title: Five killed in Lebanon as Israeli forces advance across key Litani River
item.description: Attacks come ahead of security talks between Lebanese and Israeli military delegations at the Pentagon.

item.title: Israel strikes building in Gaza’s Shati refugee camp
item.description: Israel strikes building in Gaza’s Shati refugee camp.

item.title: Eid and sacrifice in Gaza
item.description: Eid al-Adha in Gaza this year marked the third Eid in a row without animal sacrifice or Hajj.

item.title: On the road to Rogun Dam, Tajikistan’s ‘greatest dream’
item.description: Al Jazeera visits the site of the Rogun dam construction, Tajikistan's 'Project of the Century'.

item.title: Colombia’s ‘Total Peace’ plan: A failure or unfinished business for Petro?
item.description: As Colombia prepares to elect a new president, candidates are debating the legacy of Gustavo Petro's signature policy.

item.title: US federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s ‘anti-weaponisation fund’
item.description: The nearly $1.8bn fund stemmed from a recent IRS settlement with Donald Trump over his leaked tax records.

item.title: PSG vs Arsenal: Champions League final – teams, start time, lineups
item.description: PSG seek to defend the title they won for the first time, while Arsenal hope for first Champions League trophy lift.

item.title: Just what are Israel’s long-term plans for Gaza?
item.description: Despite an apparent ceasefire, Israel's leaders continue to hint at annexation and ethnic cleansing.

item.title: Eight girls arrested on suspicion of arson after Kenya school fire
item.description: The fire broke out at the Utumishi Girls Academy Senior School in Gilgil, killing 16 people and wounding 79 others.

item.title: Kambo: What is the frog poison ‘spiritual ritual’ killing detoxers?
item.description: Indigenous groups have warned against the use of kambo by non-specialist practitioners.

item.title: Sudan medical group says RSF-affiliated fighters kill 27 civilians
item.description: The attacks come as nearly 19.5 million Sudanese face severe hunger amid the country’s deepening humanitarian crisis.

item.title: QUAD to build Fiji port: A new US-China flashpoint?
item.description: Quad kicks back to life in Asia Pacific, looking to develop ports in Fiji, in a counter to China's rise.

item.title: Following Syria’s broken tracks from Baniyas to Aleppo
item.description: From Baniyas to Aleppo, a 24-hour freight train journey reveals Syria’s shattered rail network and resilient workers.

item.title: Israeli plan to extend control in Gaza provokes German concern
item.description: Comments mark a shift in Berlin’s stance towards one of its key allies, of which it has long been a strong supporter.

item.title: Harvard Medical School graduate dedicates speech to Lebanon and Palestine
item.description: Harvard Medical School graduate Leen Ezzeddine dedicated her graduation speech to Palestine and Lebanon.

item.title: Firefighters work to free riders stuck atop a rollercoaster in Texas
item.description: Firefighters work to free riders stuck atop a rollercoaster in Texas

item.title: UN urges ‘urgent’ action to protect children online
item.description: Call comes amid a global push for greater accountability and oversight of social media platforms.

item.title: World Cup 2026: How France created football’s deepest talent pool
item.description: France overcame decades of underachievement to create a football talent system unmatched anywhere in the world.

item.title: French prosecutors to probe Israeli treatment of flotilla activists
item.description: Foreign minister asserts abuse against French nationals likely to amount to criminal offences if proven.

item.title: Australian court sets August date for ‘mushroom murderer’ appeal hearing
item.description: Erin Patterson was found guilty of killing three family members as she served them a lunch laced with poisonous fungi.
